title: Meibomia exigua

taxonomy: Meibomia exigua is a species within the genus Meibomia, which is a part of the legume family (Fabaceae). The Meibomia genus is often referred to as the Bush Clover genus, and it is native to North America. Meibomia exigua is typically classified as a flowering plant, specifically a perennial herb. This species is often found growing in open woods and along roadsides. Meibomia exigua is closely related to other species within the Fabaceae family, which includes beans, peas, and clover.
uses: Meibomia exigua, also known as dwarf meadow-beauty, is a plant species used in traditional medicine. Its medicinal uses include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The plant's leaves and stems are often used in teas and infusions to alleviate these symptoms.
